description:
Align the delivery plugin's prime.post re-materialization with the §16 doctor hook's store-dir source, ahead of the frozen-arch removal of 'operating'.

CONTEXT. The frozen architecture (docs/architecture.md, bl-cac0/bl-7d46) §15 config/store-split resolution states verbatim: the split 'DISSOLVES the whole trail/terminus/operating/stealth-mode apparatus.' §1 verbatim: 'There is NO operating/ symlink and no terminus to resolve.' So wire Binding's 'operating' field is slated for deletion under epic bl-72a8 build-corrections.

CURRENT STATE. src/bin/bl-delivery.rs fn prime() locates the store checkout via wire.binding.operating (added in bl-3d75). The newer §16 doctor hook (bl-0ec3, fn doctor() in the same file) has identical actor-scoped diffless semantics but reads the store from env::current_dir() instead, commented 'operating/ — the diffless cwd balls invokes us in'. Plugins are spawned with current_dir(operating) (src/plugin.rs:187) and checkout::run_chain passes operating to Engine::diffless, so for a diffless op env::current_dir() == binding.operating TODAY. The switch is behavior-neutral now.

CHANGE. In fn prime(): replace the wire.binding.operating.ok_or_else(...) lookup with env::current_dir()?, matching fn doctor(). Removes prime's dependency on a field the migration deletes; makes prime/doctor consistent (one store-dir source). ~2 lines + test fixture (the prime e2e currently passes operating on the wire; switch it to run the binary with cwd at the store dir, as the doctor test does). Keep 100% coverage; the 'missing binding.operating' error arm and its test go away with the field dependency.

RELATES bl-3d75 (landed the prime handler), bl-0ec3 (the doctor hook that established the cwd source).
title: Delivery prime.post: read store from cwd, not wire.binding.operating
